concepts.name: Platforms as networked value creation
concepts.definition: The book's core discipline for platform strategy: a way to structure attention, choices, and action around the central mechanism described in this chapter.

concepts.name: Chapter-Specific Framework
concepts.definition: Platform metrics differ fundamentally from pipeline metrics. Chapter introduces platform-specific KPIs: liquidity (how quickly and successfully value units are matched), match quality, trust and reputation scores, multi-homing rates, network effect strength (retention by cohort, organic vs. paid acquisition ratio), and platform's take rate. Warning against applying pipeline metrics to platforms: measuring 'efficiency' without measuring 'effectiveness of matches' leads to optimizing the wrong thing.

concepts.name: Decision Discipline
concepts.definition: A practical logic for turning analysis into choices, signals, tests, governance, or commitments rather than leaving insight as narrative.

concepts.name: System Effects
concepts.definition: The recognition that outcomes emerge from interacting forces, participants, incentives, feedback loops, or external changes rather than from one isolated variable.

arguments.claim: The framework changes how managers see the system
arguments.evidence: The chapter gives managers a vocabulary and sequence for seeing hidden structure: forces and signals in scenario work, or participants and interactions in platform work.

arguments.claim: The value appears only when insight changes action
arguments.evidence: The chapter repeatedly links diagnosis to decisions, indicators, governance, metrics, launch choices, or strategic commitments.

cases.name: Framework application case
cases.context: The chapter applies platform strategy to a concrete managerial problem.
cases.illustrates: How the framework reveals hidden structure, trade-offs, and action points.
cases.lesson: Use the method to change decisions, not merely to label a situation.

logicChains.description: Define the central decision → map forces/participants and uncertainties → identify the key mechanism → test implications or interactions → monitor signals and adapt action.
logicChains.sourceHint: Synthesized from source chapter and index summary.

terminology.definition: Core idea of this platform strategy chapter.
An observable indicator that a future, network state, or strategic condition is emerging.
A reinforcing or balancing mechanism that changes outcomes over time.

nuance: The method is not prediction; it is structured preparation and action under uncertainty.
The framework must be revisited as signals, participants, and conditions change.
